title: Survival run_loop has no MT5 reconnection / per-iteration error handling
source: Code audit 2026-06-15 (live execution core + launch subagents), finding C2
why: scripts/run_survival_live.py:706-738 run_loop's `while True` only catches KeyboardInterrupt. Any transient MT5 exception (disconnect, terminal restart, expired session) propagates from risk_check/sched.tick and kills the process silently. No backoff, no broker.connect() retry, no per-iteration try/except. Over a frozen 5-day unsupervised run a transient MT5 disconnect leaves the intraday kill-switch not executing and positions unmanaged until forced liquidation — the exact scenario the risk layer exists to prevent. The stale-log issue (.bat doesn't redirect stdout) makes the death invisible except via the PID dashboard.
value_rationale: The intraday kill-switch is the primary capital backstop; if the loop dies it stops running. Single highest correctness risk for an unsupervised frozen run. High.
scope: Wrap each run_loop iteration in try/except with exponential backoff.
On MT5 errors, attempt broker.connect()/reinitialize before retrying; cap retries then escalate (notify).
Restart is safe (idempotent scheduler cursor + persisted latches) — verify a reconnect resumes without double-trade.
Test: simulated transient MT5BrokerError does not kill the loop; reconnect resumes.
recommendation: Per-iteration try/except + bounded reconnect. Pairs with B0176 (watchdog) for full resilience.

history.note: From hackathon code audit, finding C2

Also (risk-officer caveat): lottery_envelope_dd_frac default 1.0->0.5 + new --lottery-envelope-dd-frac flag; documented daily-kill UTC auto-resume under freeze in runbook 3.3/6.2. 6 new tests, suite 2208 passed.
